column3: Alice would love to join to help overcome shyness and anxiety
Alice is currently out of school due to anxiety
Extremely limited vision in left eye
Arlo has mild cerebral palsy - left sided weakness in arm and leg
None other than we would prefer myself or Bellas mother be present (in view)

column9: if anyone in her class is on this day
column15: maybe if any from her class sign up for this

column3: Casper has a visual impairment which means he is very short sighted, lacks some peripheral vision and struggles in low / no light situations. It generally doesn’t affect him day to day, but he is at higher risk of complications from head bumps or falls. Please do let us know if he has any bumps in class.

Charle was born with Nystagmus, a brain condition that affects his eye sight. He wears glasses and can see very well. His eyes wobble from side to side, this does not stop Charlie from doing anything.
He has asthma so has an inhaler in his bag. Rarely needs it though.
ADHD (not medicated), Autism and dyspraxia. He currently has an EHCP plan
Anxiety around winged insects indoors and outdoors.
Occasional dizziness due to low iron
Ellie is adopted- happy for family sharing of film shoot as long as this then isn't shared on Facebook
Ellie needs lots of reassurance sometimes or will worry a lot she's no good at things.
Emmy has Tourette’s Syndrome, usually fairly contained with medication. But has OCD and is most likely ASD (long wait for diagnosis). Sensory issues surrounding hair/ touch sometimes.
Fliss has ADHD and RSD, she is also intolerant to diary

Congenital heart condition , tricuspid atresia and VSD . Last open heart surgery age 4 . Prescribed aspirin and lisinopril daily . If trauma injury sustained may take longer to stop bleeding due to aspirin. Can become tired with over exertion, Harry is aware of when he needs to stop and take 5minute rest

Heidi has an issue with her right foot. it can mainly cause pain and discomfort but she manages this well. Being assessed by the language and communication team.  She can be shy when meeting new people and lack confidence

Jack has Sever dyslexia he will struggle to read from script but if verbally told he will remember. Issues with fainting, will go grey, need to sit down stomach pain headache. Low blood sugar treat with full fat pop sugary snack
She is suspected dyslexic and does have some anxiety as a consequence but it makes her very expressive.. hopefully this will help in drama classes but who knows if she might be shy.

Lenny is neurodivergent - with traits of ADHD and Autism. He is also gifted. He manages extremely well but may struggle with transitions and some subtle aspects of social communication. He may need reminders when following verbal instructions as he can become distracted by his thoughts and 'busy head'.
Leo is trans male. Is diagnosed ASC which is mainly for SEMH needs

Lily has epilepsy. It’s well controlled and hasn’t had a seizure for over a year now so i’m sure everything will be ok whilst at classes.  
If anything were to happen,Support her head  Ambulance would only called if the seizure lasts for more than 5 minutes or if she’s hurt herself in the fall. Ring me 
Previous seizure she has gone into an unresponsive stare before falling.

Special educational needs: Autism with moderate learning disability. Struggles to process instructions on time. Sometimes needs extra time for instructions. Can be anxious when put on the spot. Hypermobility syndrome. Asthma.
Can be a little shy at first.
Maddie is under assessment for ADHD. She is often very happy and excited, but can have some big feelings from time to time.
mild asthma when younger but only takes inhaler when has a cough. also hayfever
She has back problems but is always fine at acting

Autism, ADHD, Moderate Hearing loss (both ears), attends SEND school, can misunderstand social situations, or mishear instructions. Regular check ins to make sure she understands what is being asked help. Can read and write confidently.
He can need support to listen effectively to others
Sam has autism and is going through an ADHD assessment soon.
Allergic to synthetic strawberry.  Asthma. Wants to pursue acting as a career. Has lots of self doubt.

ADHD and ASD. Instructions must be clear. Anxious if she, or anyone around her has nausea or vomiting. Call mum if she needs support.
Skin sensitivities to numerous hygiene, sun protection, and cosmetic products.
Nothing other than he starts off really shy and then usually comes into his own after a little while.
